target: Part 2b §2b.1 stratification paragraph — replace hard-coded WIN-ID lists and verdict counts with templated placeholders so values stay in sync with wins.json on each build
patch_target_node: part2b.html — stratification paragraph (starts "Stratifying the unmonitored WINs:")
replacement_html:
Stratifying the unmonitored WINs: (Counts and ID lists are templated from wins.json at build time; verdicts and code-analysis tags update on each build.) The {{CA_NONE}} WINs with no monitoring code are not all equivalent failures — they break down by our verdict as follows. {{CA_NONE_UNFAL}} unfalsifiable theological/cosmological assertions with no measurable content ({{CA_NONE_UNFAL_IDS}}) — monitoring is not applicable. {{CA_NONE_SC}} self-contradicted by the dome's own geometry ({{CA_NONE_SC_IDS}}) — monitoring would expose the contradiction. {{CA_NONE_MISLEADING}} misleading (duplicated, circular, or non-discriminating; {{CA_NONE_MISLEADING_IDS}}). {{CA_NONE_STDMODEL}} explained by standard physics ({{CA_NONE_STDMODEL_IDS}}) — monitoring would confirm the phenomenon but not the dome's causal explanation. {{CA_NONE_REFUTED}} refuted by published data ({{CA_NONE_REFUTED_IDS}}) — monitoring would expose the failure. {{CA_NONE_NOTDEMO}} not demonstrated. These verdict-stratified counts sum to {{CA_NONE}} — every unmonitored WIN is accounted for.

The relabelling axis is orthogonal to the verdict split above: separately, {{CA_NONE_RELABELS}} of the {{CA_NONE}} unmonitored WINs carry the relabels_standard=true tag, meaning the dome adopts the standard-physics observation and renames the cause without deriving its own value. Combined with monitoring=none, this is the cleanest possible signature of a non-test — there is no validation code, and the prediction is a rebadge of an existing answer. The gaps are systematic: the dome's monitoring avoids exactly the categories where testing would be most damaging.

required_template_additions.description: These six count placeholders and six matching ID-list placeholders must be wired into build-scripts/generate-html.js and build-scripts/section-loader.js at the same site where {{CA_NONE}} and {{CA_HARDCODED}} are defined.

placeholders.{{CA_NONE_UNFAL_IDS}}: comma-joined formatted WIN-IDs of CA_NONE Unfalsifiable subset (build helper: list_of_winids(filter))
placeholders.{{CA_NONE_STDMODEL_IDS}}: same pattern, Std Model Explains subset
placeholders.{{CA_NONE_REFUTED_IDS}}: same pattern, Refuted by Data subset

required_template_additions.id_format: Recommend rendering each ID as "WIN-NNN" comma-joined (e.g., "WIN-023, WIN-032") for screen-reader friendliness; use " and " before the final ID when N≤4, plain commas when N>4.
required_template_additions.build_invariant: Add a build-time assertion: sum of CA_NONE_{UNFAL,SC,MISLEADING,STDMODEL,REFUTED,NOTDEMO} must equal {{CA_NONE}} — fail the build if not. This is the curmudgeon's explicit recommendation and prevents this drift from recurring silently.

current_values_at_authoring.note: These match wins.json at commit ba9a149. They will refresh on each build via the template placeholders above.

holes_addressed: critical hole 1: WIN-034 listed as Unfalsifiable theological — actually Self-Contradicted; correct set is {023, 032}

critical hole 1: "50 are standard-physics relabellings" was both axis-confused (relabels_standard is a flag, not a verdict) and numerically wrong (49 total reviewed; 28 within CA_NONE) — moved to a separate orthogonal-axis paragraph
critical hole 1: "Two are not demonstrated" — actual 0 (the only Not Demonstrated WIN, 058b, is filtered out of baseWins by build.js)
critical hole 1: Std Model Explains category (11 CA_NONE WINs) missing entirely from prior stratification — added as its own line
critical hole 1: prose stratification arithmetic visibly failed (2+7+15+3+2 = 29 ≠ 43); template-driven counts make sum-to-CA_NONE guaranteed by construction
new_evidence_added: wins.json at HEAD (commit ba9a149) — canonical per-WIN code_analysis.monitoring and verdict tags
curmudgeon current_counts_snapshot in monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/HOLISTIC-HOL-COMPLETENESS.c3.json — independently computed by curmudgeon and cross-verified against my re-tally

anticipated_objections.objection: Why use template placeholders rather than hard-coded values? The values are what they are at build time.
anticipated_objections.response: Hard-coded values are exactly how this drift accumulated. The original paragraph was correct in a prior cycle, then wins.json verdicts shifted (WIN-034 from theological to self-contradicted), reviewed counts grew (CA_NONE 29→43), and the prose stayed frozen. Templates with a build-time sum-check assertion fail loudly on drift rather than silently misleading readers. The curmudgeon explicitly recommends this approach.

anticipated_objections.objection: The orthogonal-axis paragraph (relabels) breaks the rhetorical flow — the original was a single punchy sentence.
anticipated_objections.response: The original sentence was also factually wrong (50 ≠ 49 ≠ 28) AND mixed two different axes (verdict vs flag). The split costs one extra paragraph but removes a category error a flat-earth-skeptical reader would otherwise spot in the source data. The kernel of truth — that relabelling+unmonitored is the cleanest non-test signature — survives, just stated more precisely.

anticipated_objections.objection: You're trusting that build.js will be wired correctly for the new placeholders before this lands.
anticipated_objections.response: Yes — the EXP requires the placeholder additions to be implemented as part of integration. The required_template_additions block above specifies the exact filter expressions and the build-time invariant (sum-check). The decider integrates the prose change and the build.js wiring together, or routes the build.js half to tinker if needed. The current_values_at_authoring block lets reviewers spot-check that the templates resolve to the correct numbers at this snapshot.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why bother with ID-list placeholders — a reader doesn't care about specific WIN numbers in a stratification paragraph.
anticipated_objections.response: The ID lists serve two purposes: (1) they let curmudgeons and integrity audits spot-check that the prose categories actually match the data without re-running the stratification themselves; (2) they make every claim auditable — a reader who wants to verify "WIN-034 is Self-Contradicted, not theological" can click through to the WIN entry. The lists are slightly verbose but the alternative is the exact failure mode that produced this hole — claims without verifiable referents.

kernel_of_truth: The dome author's monitoring code genuinely IS structured the way our analysis describes — monitoring=none WINs really do skew toward verdict categories where testing would expose problems, and relabelling really does pile on top of monitoring=none to produce the cleanest "non-test" signature. Our prior prose got the structural argument right; what failed was keeping the specific counts and ID lists in sync with wins.json as we extended the review. The fix preserves the kernel and makes drift impossible going forward.
summary_for_decider: Stratification paragraph in §2b.1 currently lists 6 hard-coded counts that all disagree with wins.json; arithmetic visibly fails (29 vs 43). Replacement uses 6 verdict-bucket template placeholders + 5 ID-list placeholders + 1 relabels-count placeholder, splits the relabels axis into its own paragraph (it was conflated with verdict buckets), and adds the missing "Std Model Explains" category. Required: add 12 new template placeholders to generate-html.js and section-loader.js per the required_template_additions block, plus a sum-check build-time assertion. Tests should verify the assertion fires on a mock drift. EXP-442 (contrast paragraph) is the adjacent §2b.1 fix and will reuse the SC_TOTAL/SC_MON_* template family; recommend processing in the same decider cycle.
